summary: Stancil argues that even if the AI boom is real at the aggregate level, the gains and disappointments will be uneven, and many startup workers grinding through 996 schedules will end up with nothing. He draws a parallel to earlier “temporary” career detours in finance/consulting and warns that there is no such thing as borrowed time—when bubbles burst, the only durable value is the human memory of what people sacrificed.
money_quotes: The AI bubble will burst—it’s just that the disappointment won’t be evenly distributed.
Some people get rich, and some people just get dirty.
You can live your life on borrowed money, but there is no such thing as borrowed time.
Sometimes, 996 adds up to generational wealth. Sometimes, it adds up to zero.
When a bubble bursts, and people’s work gets erased and their hours wasted, all that remains is what other people witnessed.
key_insight: The AI gold rush may enrich a few, but for many it will trade irreplaceable time for nothing, so the humane response is to witness and honor those sacrifices.
